title: Semantic multi-page site deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Original misconception and checkpoint items retained; executable evidence is intentionally absent because A coherent multi-page site requires multi-file navigation, content-quality, and human usability review rather than one isolated code item.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent standards, browser, and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: Human review must verify platform-version accuracy, equivalence, accessibility, and deterministic grading before formal use.
items.title: Semantic multi-page site: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents misconception? If each page validates alone, the complete site's navigation and user journeys are automatically correct.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Verify the complete deployed site, including cross-page navigation, paths, consistency, content, and task flows.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Verify the complete deployed site, including cross-page navigation, paths, consistency, content, and task flows.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Verify the complete deployed site, including cross-page navigation, paths, consistency, content, and task flows.
